description: The life story of Malcolm X, told to Alex Haley, tracing his journey from prison to the Nation of Islam and Pan-Africanism.
title: The Autobiography of Malcolm X

description: King's account of the 1963 Birmingham campaign, including the Letter from Birmingham Jail and the case for nonviolent direct action.
subjects: Civil rights;Civil rights movements;African American history;Social justice
title: Why We Can't Wait

description: An epic history of the Great Migration of six million Black Americans out of the South, told through three lives.
subjects: Great Migration;African American history;African Americans;Race relations
title: The Warmth of Other Suns

description: An argument that mass incarceration functions as a contemporary system of racial control in the United States.
title: The New Jim Crow

description: Hundreds of Americans talk in their own words about the jobs they do all day and how they feel about them.
title: Working

description: An oral history of the Great Depression assembled from the recollections of those who lived through it.
title: Hard Times: An Oral History of the Great Depression

description: The first volume of a narrative history of the civil rights movement centered on Martin Luther King Jr.
subjects: Civil rights movements;Civil rights;African American history;American history
title: Parting the Waters: America in the King Years 1954-63

description: An undercover account of trying to survive on low-wage jobs in America at the turn of the century.
title: Nickel and Dimed: On (Not) Getting By in America

description: A landmark collection of essays on race, the color line, and Black identity in post-Reconstruction America.
subjects: African American history;Race relations;African Americans;Social justice
title: The Souls of Black Folk

description: A sweeping history of the American labor movement from the early mills to the modern era.
subjects: Labor unions;Labor history;Strikes and lockouts;Trade unions
title: There Is Power in a Union

description: A reporter returns to his collapsing hometown to chronicle the decline of Detroit and its people.
title: Detroit: An American Autopsy

description: A labor lawyer's wry, impassioned account of representing unions in an age of their decline.
title: Which Side Are You On?: Trying to Be for Labor When It's Flat on Its Back

description: King's first-hand account of the Montgomery bus boycott and the birth of nonviolent mass protest.
subjects: Civil rights movements;Civil rights;African American history;Sermons
title: Stride Toward Freedom: The Montgomery Story

description: A history of Black and white radicals who organized in Depression-era Alabama against great odds.
subjects: Labor history;African American history;Social justice;Strikes and lockouts
title: Hammer and Hoe: Alabama Communists During the Great Depression

description: A study of how race, housing, and deindustrialization shaped inequality in postwar Detroit.
subjects: Detroit (Mich.) history;Race relations;African American history;Labor history
title: The Origins of the Urban Crisis: Race and Inequality in Postwar Detroit

description: A study of the League of Revolutionary Black Workers and Detroit's insurgent labor movement.
subjects: Detroit (Mich.) history;Labor history;African American history;Strikes and lockouts
title: Detroit: I Do Mind Dying

description: A theological meditation linking the cross of Christ to the history of lynching in America.
title: The Cross and the Lynching Tree

description: A definitive biography of King and the SCLC and their leadership of the civil rights movement.
subjects: Biography;Civil rights movements;Civil rights;African American history
title: Bearing the Cross: Martin Luther King, Jr., and the Southern Christian Leadership Conference

description: A narrative biography tracing the life and ministry of Martin Luther King Jr.
title: Let the Trumpet Sound: A Life of Martin Luther King, Jr.

description: A history of the long Black struggle for freedom from the slave ships through emancipation.
subjects: African American history;Social justice;Race relations;Civil rights
title: There Is a River: The Black Struggle for Freedom in America

description: American history retold from the perspective of working people, labor, and the marginalized.
title: A People's History of the United States

description: A cultural history of Motown Records set against the racial politics of postwar Detroit.
title: Dancing in the Street: Motown and the Cultural Politics of Detroit

description: A devotional and theological work on what Christianity offers to those with their backs against the wall.
title: Jesus and the Disinherited

description: The memoir of gospel legend Mahalia Jackson, from New Orleans to the heights of American music.
title: Movin' On Up

description: A history of the 1970s tracing the unraveling of the American working class and its political world.
title: Stayin' Alive: The 1970s and the Last Days of the Working Class
